title: Corning Inc. (GLW) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Corning Inc (GLW).

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 6% free cash flow growth rate acknowledges the cyclical nature of Corning's business, balancing the sluggish legacy telecom environment against the high-growth potential of optical connectivity for AI data centers.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9% discount rate is utilized to reflect the inherent cyclicality and capital intensity of Corning's operations, as well as its exposure to volatile end-markets like consumer electronics.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal growth rate aligns with long-term inflation and modest GDP growth, appropriate for a mature, capital-intensive manufacturing enterprise.

thesisTitle: The Backbone of the AI Revolution
thesisParagraphs: While historically known for consumer glass and traditional telecom fiber, Corning is rapidly positioning itself as a critical enabler of the artificial intelligence boom. The massive data centers required to train and run large language models demand unprecedented levels of high-density optical connectivity, an area where Corning is the undisputed technological leader.

However, this powerful secular tailwind is somewhat obscured by the inherent cyclicality of its other business units, such as display panels and traditional telecom infrastructure. While the AI thesis is strong, investors must be prepared to weather the cyclical fluctuations inherent in a capital-intensive materials science business.
verdictFooter: Based on current market conditions. Data via Finviz/SEC EDGAR (Mar 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 6% growth rate for Corning?
faq.answer: The 6% rate balances the strong growth trajectory of its AI-driven optical communications segment against the slower, cyclical performance of its display and traditional telecom businesses.

faq.question: How does AI impact Corning's valuation?
faq.answer: AI data centers require massive amounts of advanced optical fiber to handle enormous data loads. As a leading manufacturer of these components, Corning is well-positioned to see significant revenue growth from this infrastructure build-out.

faq.question: Is Corning currently overvalued?
faq.answer: Based on our DCF model, Corning is currently trading very close to its intrinsic value, making it a 'Fair Value' holding rather than a deep-value play.
